item.title: Before her alleged murder, Tunchanok Donhomla smiled through 'tough life'
item.description: While the public will remember Thai teenager Tunchanok Donhomla for the tragic nature of her death, her aunt, Mee Boonsert, will remember the baby she helped raise.

item.title: Lucas Herrington has 'really bright future' as Socceroos rally around the 18yo following World Cup exit
item.description: Lucas Herrington took, and missed, Australia's final penalty in its World Cup loss to Egypt. His teammates having nothing but praise for the teenager.

item.title: Wimbledon shock as Filipino Alexandra Eala makes history against Iga Świątek
item.description: Alexandra Eala is left in tears after her stunning victory over reigning Wimbledon champion Iga Świątek.

item.title: How Peter accidentally discovered the SS Gothenburg and 'all the bits of a great shipwreck story'
item.description: The startling noise of metal on metal was unusual on a coral reef and unlocked a nearly century-old mystery of a tragic maritime disaster, sunken treasure and a peculiar, inscribed turtle shell.

item.title: Dentists see complications after Australians travel overseas for treatments they cannot afford here
item.description: Janelle Jeal thought having dental care overseas would be life changing. It was, but for the wrong reasons.

item.title: Meet Mulga Bore Hard Rock, the Indigenous desert cousins keeping the spirit of AC/DC alive
item.description: Inspired by AC/DC and raised in a remote NT community, these young cousins have become one of Australia's most unlikely hard rock bands.

item.title: Back fracture sparks AFL national draft prospect Jobe Janeway's footy career
item.description: Rather than ending his promising sporting prospects, a fractured back has prompted Mount Gambier tall Jobe Janeway to chase his dream of playing in the AFL.

item.title: How NAIDOC grew from a one-day protest to a week-long celebration
item.description: Traditionally, NAIDOC Week was celebrated by First Nations people, but it has become an invitation for non-Indigenous Australians to get involved.

item.title: Japan's LGBTQIA+ education plan ineffective and 'non-binding', experts say
item.description: The first national plan of its kind is broadly criticised by gay rights proponents and legal experts for supporting the status quo.

item.title: Greyhound injuries, deaths kept offline out of 'respect' for trainers and 'their animals', Tasracing says
item.description: If you watched greyhound race videos on Tasracing's website, you would likely never know that dozens of dogs were injured or killed during events last year.

item.title: Prince Harry will not be joined by Meghan and two children on London visit
item.description: UK media report the decision was taken after a request for police protection was denied.

item.title: Fran Lebowitz doesn't have a smartphone
item.description: No-one emerges unscathed from Fran Lebowitz's quips, bon mots and scathing take-downs of New York's peculiarities and poor etiquette.

item.title: Nhulunbuy housing provider urged to get back to basics amid post-mining planning crunch
item.description: A government-funded housing provider in the Northern Territory mining town of Nhulunbuy has been criticised for "not doing enough" to support small business owners.

item.title: Memorable stories from Jackie Chan filming First Strike in Falls Creek in the 1990s
item.description: Thirty years ago a Hollywood crew turned Falls Creek into Ukraine, and Jackie Chan nearly froze getting the shot.

item.title: Australians cagey on Ellyse Perry injury ahead of women's T20 World Cup final
item.description: The women's T20 World Cup final looms in what could be Ellyse Perry's last match for Australia, and an injury cloud hangs over the champion.

item.title: World Cup: France finally breaks free of stubborn Paraguay on Independence Day
item.description: After strolling through the World Cup so far, France faced its first major test against the determined defence of Paraguay. In stifling heat and with tensions boiling over, Les Bleus came through largely unscathed.

item.title: Maintenance issues, outages plague WA standalone power systems
item.description: Farmers in WA's south say the standalone power systems they got from Western Power after being moved off the grid have been problematic.

item.title: International experts say surveilling local birds, marine mammals a 'priority' in bird flu response
item.description: While the H5N1 bird flu strain is relatively new to Australia, experts who have monitored the spread in other continents say surveillance is key to tackling the deadly virus.

item.title: Banker-turned-barefoot mosaic artist and ceramicist spend six years recreating Klimt's The Kiss
item.description: When a bank manager discovered mosaics, he threw away his shoes and tie and vowed never to wear them again.

item.title: World Cup quick hits: Mo Salah shows his class to Socceroos, Paraguay labelled a 'disgrace'
item.description: Mo Salah's classy post-penalty act to the stricken Socceroos and a Paraguay team blasted as a "disgrace". Here are today's World Cup quick hits.

item.title: Liberal frontbencher Dan Tehan says coalition with One Nation not 'being talked about'
item.description: Shadow energy minister Dan Tehan says the Liberal party is not entertaining thoughts of forming a coalition with One Nation.

item.title: Geraldton band Ratsalad gains global renown with Jackass movie song
item.description: Geraldton band Ratsalad thought they were being scammed when the producer of the latest Jackass movie said they wanted to feature their song in the credits.

item.title: Love story: Finding love after loss, at the dog park
item.description: Jorgen and Jill Porse were in the depths of grief when they first met at their local dog park 15 years ago.

item.title: Researchers call for rare WA reptiles to be listed as endangered
item.description: New research has revealed the Pilbara barking gecko and Bungle Bungle robust slider are facing a heightened risk of extinction due to a rapidly warming climate.

item.title: Father, daughter sail Newcastle to Norway returning yacht to its roots
item.description: A NSW family is returning a wooden yacht to its roots in Norway, but it has not been all smooth sailing.

item.title: For many football fans, this World Cup has been one of exclusion and travel bans
item.description: There's never been a more diverse World Cup in history, but the 2026 version has also been one of exclusion for many fans wanting to join in the fun.

item.title: Statue of former St Kilda player Nicky Winmar removed from Perth Stadium after assault conviction
item.description: A statue immortalising a key moment in AFL history against racism is taken down, after its subject Nicky Winmar is found guilty of assaulting a woman.

item.title: NRL tries Hail Mary for Swift and Kelce after Knights end Dolphins' win streak
item.description: The NRL shoots its shot with the most famous couple in the world, suggesting Taylor Swift and NFL star Travis Kelce might want to attend next year's season openers in Las Vegas.

item.title: Trump commemorates 250th US Independence Day after storms force evacuation of National Mall
item.description: The occasion was complicated by severe storms that gathered near Washington, forcing event organisers to order an earlier evacuation.

item.title: Power surge home to down Roos, rulings handed down for Suns-Pies melee
item.description: Port Adelaide completes a hard-fought 21-point win over North Melbourne, St Kilda runs riot against Essendon, and the AFL fines rather than bans Brayden Maynard and Touk Miller for making contact with an umpire.

item.title: Magnitude-3.8 earthquake rocks coastal community along Victoria's Great Ocean Road
item.description: The magnitude-3.8 earthquake struck at a depth of 10 kilometres along Victoria's popular Great Ocean Road.

item.title: Suspected space junk washes up on north Queensland beach
item.description: Authorities are trying to determine how to safely contain a growing cluster of mysterious space debris that keeps washing ashore on a north Queensland beach.

item.title: Epping footballer in end-of-life care after head clash during Melbourne game
item.description: Epping Football Netball Club's Nathan Fitzgerald, 27, is receiving end-of-life care after colliding with a teammate in a reserves game.

item.title: Australia defeats England to win ICC Women's T20 Cricket World Cup
item.description: Australia has won the ICC Women's T20 World Cup in commanding style, defeating England by seven wickets in the final at Lord's.

item.title: This week in history quiz, July 6: Who quit Pearl Jam last year and what do you know about Wimbledon?
item.description: Let us test your smarts about Captain James Cook's last voyage, the debut release of a popular British girl group and other historical events.

item.title: The best way to break screen-time rules for your kids when you need to
item.description: Screen time has become a phrase that makes "parents feel instantly guilty", but how detrimental is a full day of screens on the odd occasion, like during school holidays or when you are home sick?

item.title: Race Around the World recap: Catch the week five films
item.description: From manga to life-sized dolls, catch up on the films from week five of Race Around the World.

item.title: The Imperial Presidency: How Donald Trump's second term is changing the White House forever
item.description: Donald Trump is undertaking regime change, not just in Venezuela, but on US soil and it is unlikely the presidency will ever be the same.

item.title: The economy is strong yet consumer sentiment is rock bottom
item.description: The paradox of a strong economy and stock market with very low consumer sentiment is not close to being resolved. Quite the opposite, the disconnect is, if anything, widening.

item.title: Gutsy England prevails over Mexico in instant World Cup classic
item.description: Ten-man England holds on to defeat Mexico 3-2 in a World Cup classic at the Azteca, with Jude Bellingham's brace and Harry Kane's penalty enough to send the Three Lions through.

item.title: Record number of exchange traded funds hit share market, but investors warn of 'gambling' risk
item.description: A record number of exchange traded funds were added to the Australian share market in the past financial year.

item.title: AFP to push Australia's Pacific police training model to reduce UN costs
item.description: The AFP commissioner will promote Pacific police training as a model for the United Nations when she addresses police delegates from 140 nations this week.

item.title: ABC trials AI writing tools for news staff amid trust warnings
item.description: Experts and the journalists’ union have welcomed the ABC’s plans to use artificial intelligence responsibly, but warned misuse could damage trust in the public broadcaster.

item.title: World Cup: How man behind Norway's viral Viking row turned team motivation into global sensation
item.description: Ole Frøystad has always been a passionate football fan and when Norway qualified for its first men's World Cup since 1998, he was inspired to create the iconic chant that's become a global sensation.

item.title: Domestic and family violence victim-survivors at risk of CTE, experts warn
item.description: Enduring brutal physical abuse can increase the risk of developing a sinister brain disease, yet many victim-survivors are unaware, experts warn.

item.title: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese signs Ocean of Peace Alliance with Fiji
item.description: Australia and Fiji strike a new defence alliance in a major development that could reshape the Pacific's strategic landscape.

item.title: SA Police report to coroner into Somerton Man case complete 'very soon'
item.description: The man who worked out the identity of the Somerton Man through a DNA match says SA Police has told him it is close to finishing its report for the coroner to finally officially put the 78-year mystery to rest.

item.title: Hospital patients given new way to escalate care concerns in South Australia
item.description: After Ryan Bowman died at the Mount Gambier Hospital, his family said their concerns over his deteriorating health went unheard.

item.title: World Cup: US striker Folarin Balogun freed to play Belgium despite red card after Donald Trump call
item.description: US striker Folarin Balogun is free to play Belgium despite earning a red card in his last match, in a shock decision that delights Donald Trump and "astonishes" the Belgians.

item.title: Mental health service supports new NT parents struggling with depression and anxiety
item.description: Natalya Wallace thought she just had the baby blues as a result of a traumatic birth experience. When she realised she needed mental health support, the young mother was relieved to find a new service in her rural area.

item.title: China to test ballistic missile in Pacific — as it happened
item.description: The Chinese government informed governments in the region about the test earlier today, the ABC has been told.

item.title: Heritage sheep breeder, 11, helping Hampshire Down variety avoid extinction
item.description: Mitch Dickens's passion for sheep genetics saw his parents give him his own stud, which they hope will help keep a heritage breed alive.

item.title: Wimbledon 2026: Naomi Osaka stuns Aryna Sabalenka to advance to quarterfinals
item.description: Naomi Osaka downs top seed Aryna Sabalenka in straight sets in a fourth round meeting of grand slam champions at Wimbledon.

item.title: Mount Isa barber leaves behind fresh fades and more on Mornington Island
item.description: Mount Isa barber Jon James travels to remote Mornington Island to teach barbering skills, hoping to leave behind a trade that will last long after he returns home.

item.title: Anthony Albanese 'unequivocally' apologises for saying he would 'shag' Kylie Minogue during podcast game
item.description: Anthony Albanese made the comments during a game of "shag, marry, date" on the Bush Deep podcast on Friday, which have been widely condemned as inappropriate.

item.title: Safety fears spark calls for better protections for Neil the seal
item.description: The elephant seal has been attracting onlookers in southern Tasmania, as well as a growing social media audience, but fears for his wellbeing and concerns that some people aren't keeping their distance have led to calls to better protect Neil.

item.title: First Nations creators tell Indigenous stories through video games
item.description: As part of NAIDOC Week, we meet the Indigenous creatives who are re-shaping Australia's video game industry.

item.title: Great Southern records highest rate of whooping cough cases in regional WA
item.description: Whooping cough cases across WA's Great Southern have already more than doubled since last year, with doctors expecting the figure to grow further.

item.title: Wimbledon 2026: Novak Djokovic beats Roger Federer record despite 'meltdowns'
item.description: Novak Djokovic fights off a plucky opponent and his own demons to etch his name into another corner of the record books at Wimbledon.

item.title: Nathan Fitzgerald's football club calls for investigation into cricket pitches on sports ovals
item.description: Nathan Fitzgerald's club has asked authorities to look at the way hard cricket pitches are covered on multi-use ovals as the 27-year-old continues to receive end-of-life care.

item.title: Developer quietly sells illegally cleared south Sydney site earmarked for now-rejected mansion
item.description: In 2020, Amir Abu Abara illegally cleared land in Barden Ridge to build a lavish mansion. After a fine, a replanting works order, and the rejection of his housing plan, he is no longer the site's owner.

item.title: Tadej Pogačar hits back at Jonas Vingegaard as Isaac del Toro claims Tour de France stage two
item.description: Tadej Pogačar and his UAE Team Emirates-XRG responded to Visma Lease a Bike's stage 1 victory with a superb one-two on stage 2 of the Tour de France.

item.title: WA minister Paul Papalia quits politics, triggering Secret Harbour by-election
item.description: WA Labor stalwart and senior state cabinet minister Paul Papalia has announced he is quitting politics after nearly two decades, triggering a by-election in his southern Perth seat.

item.title: New cancer care program empowers Yarrabah residents undergoing treatment
item.description: A new program in Far North Queensland aims to make life easier for people receiving treatment for cancer, while reducing the number of people who develop the disease.

item.title: Humpback whale freed from fishing gear on NSW Central Coast
item.description: Whale rescuers say there have been 24 entanglements already this migration season. Meanwhile, a small whale was euthanised after it stranded itself on a beach near Newcastle, despite rescue efforts from residents and experts.

item.title: Nigel Farage referred to UK standards watchdog over undeclared gifts from crypto investor George Cottrell
item.description: Nigel Farage was reportedly provided with security services, social media support and accommodation by George Cottrell, a 32-year-old crypto investor who previously served eight months in prison for wire fraud.

item.title: Australia's Space agency says beach objects likely fuel pressure vessels from launch vehicle
item.description: Australia's Space Agency says objects found on a north Queensland beach are likely pressure vessels from a space launch vehicle.

item.title: Wildcards from Whyalla return to win H2 Grand Prix after disqualification heartbreak
item.description: A year after a devastating technical disqualification snatched victory from five South Australian teens at a hydrogen-powered engineering race, they returned as wildcard entrants to the 2026 H2 Grand Prix Finals in Switzerland.

item.title: Maroons skipper Cameron Munster focused on State of Origin decider against Blues
item.description: Cameron Munster wants to honour Queensland State of Origin greats by leading the Maroons to a series victory over NSW in Brisbane on Wednesday night.

item.title: World Cup: Ice-cold Erling Haaland with the moments that matter to send Brazil crashing
item.description: Brazil has long been trying to re-capture its glory days, but they are fading further into obscurity, while Erling Haaland proves Norway is a genuine World Cup threat in 2-1 win.

item.title: SANFL avoids strike action after months of negotiations over pay
item.description: SANFL Players' Association members have voted for a memorandum of understanding with the state league amid an ongoing decline in players' pay.

item.title: Rare humpback whale calf, believed to be a leucistic, spotted off NSW and Queensland coast
item.description: The calf, thought to be one of only a dozen in the Southern Hemisphere, has been spotted swimming alongside its mother.

item.title: Sophie Molineux praised after leading Australia to Women's T20 World Cup win
item.description: Sophie Molineux is praised for her leadership ability after guiding Australia to its Women's T20 World Cup victory.

item.title: World Cup quick hits: England star hurt in celebrations, Erling Haaland surprises himself
item.description: England revels in a famous victory over Mexico, but the celebrations come at a cost for one star, while Erling Haaland's brilliance even has the man himself dumbfounded. Here are today's World Cup quick hits.

item.title: Socceroo Awer Mabil speaks on his team's multiculturalism
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: 'Destabilising' Chinese ballistic missile test in Pacific prompts government concerns
item.description: China has test-fired a long-range missile with a dummy warhead in the Pacific Ocean, prompting concerns from the Australian government.

item.title: Four of our best self-saucing puddings for winter
item.description: You'd never guess it but self-saucing puddings are so easy to make. Here are four of our best for you try while the weather is cool.

item.title: Mabil says monoculture debate 'bulls***' as Socceroos return home from FIFA World Cup
item.description: The Socceroos star says anyone "trying to divide" the community is "probably not Australian themselves".

item.title: England's World Cup Azteca thriller a stunning deliverance from ghosts of the past
item.description: England teams of old would have buckled under the pressure put upon them by Mexico and their 80,000-strong band of supporters at the Azteca. But this England team turned a nightmare into a dreamland, at a venue haunted by so many ghosts of the past.

item.title: Comedian's 'terrifying' roadside assistance ordeal sparks national change
item.description: A woman's campaign for change after her own frightening experience being broken down outside Canberra will see NRMA roadside assistance contractors required to provide a code to customers to verify their identity.

item.title: 'Assassination plot' impeachment trial involving Duterte dynasty begins in Manila
item.description: A highly consequential impeachment trial against Philippines Vice-President Sara Duterte could spell the end for her political career and shape the nation's 2028 election.

item.title: Police investigating suspicious death of 17-year-old girl in remote NT community of Galiwin'ku
item.description: Northern Territory Police say the girl was found inside a home in the remote community of Galiwin'ku, and was declared dead at the scene. A 34-year-old man known to the woman has been arrested.

item.title: Court told 'Razor' scooter used to murder axe-wielding man near Newcastle
item.description: A Supreme Court jury hears a Razor-branded scooter was the alleged murder weapon used to kill a man who was armed with an axe during a violent brawl north of Newcastle.

item.title: 'Pink army' gathers in Rundle Mall to welcome home victorious Thunderbirds
item.description: Hundreds of fans have flocked to Rundle Mall, dressed in pink, to welcome back the Adelaide Thunderbirds following an unforgettable grand final win.

item.title: New all-time Australian air pressure record under intense winter weather system
item.description: Preliminary data shows the highest ever pressure in Australia was recorded in Tasmania on Monday morning.

item.title: Edge Early Learning Gawler East closed for second time after child taken to hospital
item.description: South Australia's Education Standards Board says it has "serious concerns" about an Edge Early Learning centre after a child was taken to hospital following an allergen incident, with the centre directed to close immediately for the second time this year.

item.title: Hobart Deputy Lord Mayor Zelinda Sherlock under fire over taking office snacks
item.description: Despite her apology and the integrity commission dismissing the case, Hobart's deputy mayor is still facing backlash after she took snacks from council premises for her home office.

item.title: Central Australian tourism funding injection celebrated after several challenging years
item.description: After several challenging years for tourism operators in central Australia, there is hope an incentive of free flights for visitors and a new grant fund will supercharge tourism in the region.

item.title: Fires rage in Kyiv after Russian missile, drone attack
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Second Russian attack on Kyiv in less than a week kills dozens on eve of NATO summit
item.description: President Volodymyr ⁠Zelenskyy urges NATO to take "strong decisions" on boosting Ukraine's air defences following the strike.

item.title: Melbourne footballer Nathan Fitzgerald dies after suffering head injury during game
item.description: Footy player and teacher Nathan Fitzgerald has died after suffering critical head injuries during a reserves game in the city's north on the weekend.

item.title: China warns Australia over military alliance with Fiji that promises to 'act to meet the common danger'
item.description: China warns Australia not to harm its interests in the Pacific after Anthony Albanese strikes a landmark military alliance with Fiji.

item.title: Prince Harry and King Charles III in awkward stand-off over offer to stay at Buckingham Palace
item.description: The strained relationship between Prince Harry and his father, King Charles III, has been laid bare once again, after an awkward stand-off over accommodation at Buckingham Palace.

item.title: Hamas to dissolve Gaza emergency government but some leaders will stay to 'prevent vacuum'
item.description: Hamas officials announced the resignation of senior members of the government, but warned that some people would remain in senior positions in Gaza's administration to "prevent any vacuum that could harm our citizens".

item.title: Trump confirms he asked FIFA to review US player's red card, as UEFA says U-turn 'crossed a red line'
item.description: Football's European governing body blasts the decision to suspend a US striker's World Cup red-card ban, saying FIFA had "crossed a red line" after US President Donald Trump personally intervened in the case.

item.title: US crashes out as Belgium gets the last laugh after controversy
item.description: Belgium completes a thumping 4-1 win over the United States, who started Folarin Balogun after his controversial reprieve from FIFA.

item.title: ABC Places quiz: Where in the world are we?
item.description: Your challenge in today's ABC Places quiz is to work out the location of 10 photos from around the world. Be warned: we might try to trick you.

item.title: No subscriptions? No problems! Here are the best movies and TV series you can watch free
item.description: From Bluey in Yolŋu Matha to the first feature film directed by an Indigenous woman, there is a trove of free movies and TV to dive into this NAIDOC Week.

item.title: Use your phone for work? This is how you can claim it as a tax deduction
item.description: The cost of buying and using your own phone may be tax deductible, but the ATO has specific rules about how employees calculate these expenses.

item.title: Too cold to exercise? Here's how to start and stay on track
item.description: Experts say winter is a good time to double down on exercise rather than taking the foot off the gas.

item.title: Donald Trump teaches crypto devotees a valuable lesson
item.description: US President Donald Trump should have been a godsend to the mysterious world of cryptocurrency. Instead, it has been a godsend to him.

item.title: One in 25 teens affected by AI-assisted online sexual abuse, study finds
item.description: About one in every 25 Australians under the age of 18 has been the victim or knows a victim of artificial intelligence-assisted online sexual abuse, according to a new study.

item.title: Activists urge Australia to sign treaty to ban nuclear weapons
item.description: There are renewed calls for Australia to sign a treaty ensuring nuclear weapons can't ever be facilitated, stored or manufactured in Australia.

item.title: WorkSafe investigating after Melbourne footballer Nathan Fitzgerald suffers life-ending head injuries
item.description: Victoria's workplace safety watchdog is investigating after a suburban footy player was injured, and later died, after his head collided with a cricket pitch.

item.title: Germany cracks down on employees calling in sick by phone
item.description: Chucking a sickie is no longer as easy as a quick phone call in Germany, under new laws hoped to boost productivity. Here's how the world is responding.

item.title: When is State of Origin III? Kick-off time and how to watch Maroons vs Blues
item.description: The men's State of Origin series has gone to a decider, with Queensland and New South Wales sharing the spoils after the opening two matches. Here is what you need to know before kick-off of Game III tomorrow night.

item.title: Man behind a bold Launceston CBD development plan may have been running insolvent companies
item.description: The Tasmanian government and a local council welcomed a bold plan to transform Launceston’s CBD from a man who may have been running insolvent companies at the time, an investigation shows.

item.title: Farmer awaits cooler temperatures this week after 'uncharacteristically' mild July
item.description: As Queenslanders brace for cooler temperatures this week, farmers on the Granite Belt are welcoming single digits to ensure their crops are thriving come harvest time.

item.title: Hobart's vibrancy could take a hit as Pub Banc Group closes popular pubs, bars and clubs
item.description: There are questions around the future of Hobart's nightlife after the abrupt closure of some of the capital city's most-loved institutions due to challenging trading conditions.

item.title: World Cup: 'Shameful', incomprehensible': Severe blowback over FIFA's opaque Balogun decision
item.description: FIFA has always insisted it is politically neutral. But its integrity is now being questioned after US President Donald Trump confirmed he asked for Folarin Balogun's suspension to be reviewed.

item.title: 'Bad blood' between US and European allies as NATO summit begins
item.description: Dozens of world leaders are descending on Türkiye this week to rubber-stamp significant NATO commitments, but the dynamics on the sidelines could prove more interesting to watch.

item.title: Three of 'Croatian Six' convicted over alleged terror plot may be innocent, inquiry finds
item.description: An inquiry has raised "reasonable doubt" over the guilt of three of six Croatian men jailed in 1981 over an alleged terrorist plot to bomb several Sydney buildings in 1979.

item.title: US prosecutors brief families on long-delayed trial for accused Bali bombing terror leader
item.description: US military prosecutors reveal new details about their case against the man accused of planning the 2002 Bali bombings, telling the loved ones of Australian victims his trial could finally begin next year.

item.title: SA Museum's 'walking archive' helps First Nations people discover family history
item.description: For 28 years, Ali Abdullah-Highfold has helped thousands of Indigenous people find familial connections through his work at the South Australian Museum.

item.title: Queensland mental health services at risk from 'huge' funding shortfall
item.description: Queensland mental health community services receive funding from government departments based on an indexation rate.

item.title: Mourners throng funeral procession in Tehran for Iran's supreme leader ayatollah Ali Khamenei
item.description: The Islamic Republic's supreme leader was killed in the early days of conflict between the United States, Israel and Iran.

item.title: Alex de Minaur loses in Wimbledon fourth round to Flavio Cobolli
item.description: Alex de Minaur loses in the fourth round at Wimbledon in another match he feels he could, and maybe should, have won against ninth seed Flavio Cobolli.

item.title: Delivery drivers losing tens of thousands to phishing scam across Australia
item.description: Amidst a growing number of people taking up gig work to cope with high living costs, scammers are stealing the earnings of "already struggling" delivery drivers.

item.title: ASX falls as forecaster gives 'downbeat' assessment of Australian economy — as it happened
item.description: Deloitte Access Economics forecasts the longest stretch of sub-2 per cent growth since the early 1990s recession. Nine Entertainment retains the free-to-air rights to the National Rugby League through to 2034.

item.title: Alcoa's looming WA alumina monopoly could threaten world's only jarrah forests, environmentalists say
item.description: Alcoa's $8 billion purchase of South32 could be key to unlocking untouched areas of WA's northern jarrah forest for bauxite mining, raising the concern of environmentalists.

item.title: Alexandra Eala makes another stride for Philippines as Jasmine Paolini ends her Wimbledon run
item.description: For a nation like the Philippines, where sports stars are held on an even higher pedestal, Alexandra Eala is taking her place among the greats.

item.title: Kaniva finally connects to drinkable mains water, but not all residents want it on tap
item.description: Victoria's largest town without drinking water finally gets a new potable supply, but it has left the community that has relied on tank water divided.

item.title: Pacific leaders criticise Chinese nuclear-capable missile test as 'reckless'
item.description: Solomon Islands PM says a ballistic missile test carried out by China is "not the act of a friend", sentiments echoed by several Pacific leaders.

item.title: Paul Papalia's difficult departure leaves WA Labor nervous about One Nation's surge
item.description: Paul Papalia's resignation for family reasons sparks a by-election in the WA minister and Labor stalwart's southern Perth seat at a time when disgruntled voters are increasingly turning to One Nation.

item.title: Ex-champion boxer charged over four kidnappings in Sydney
item.description: Robbery and Serious Crime Squad detectives have charged 26-year-old Humzah Khalid, previously known by his boxing name The Raging Bull, for his alleged involvement in four kidnappings over 10 months.

item.title: Retired judge Christopher Beale reflects on Erin Patterson murder trial in podcast interview
item.description: Retired judge Christopher Beale has reflected on his long legal career, his temporary pivot to becoming a Jesuit priest and the role he played in the Erin Patterson murder trial.

item.title: Cuba accuses US of 'genocide' amid humanitarian crisis and Trump threats
item.description: The lives of ordinary Cubans have ground to a halt under an economic and humanitarian crisis that has developed over years and been aggravated by US President Donald Trump's harsh restrictions, experts say.

item.title: Family of Gavin Feltwell call for stronger sentencing for mining corporations
item.description: They waited four years for a court outcome, only to be left feeling like it was an injustice.

item.title: Democrats' Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner accused of sexual assault
item.description: Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner says the allegation is "categorically false", but is considering his next steps as the report prompts several key backers to withdraw their endorsements.

item.title: Baby lamb twin boom brings hope to sheep farmer after years of dry
item.description: A wet start to 2026 in regional South Australia has helped create perfect conditions for multiple lamb births on one merino sheep stud.

item.title: Australia's Indian community prepares to put on a show for Prime Minister Narendra Modi
item.description: On a grey July day, a Melbourne community hall is bursting with colour as Australia's Indian community gears up to welcome Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi.

item.title: Bryce Cotton stars in Boomers' basketball World Cup qualifying against Philippines
item.description: Six-time NBL MVP Bryce Cotton shines as he pulls on Australian colours for the first time in the Boomers' FIBA World Cup qualifiers.

item.title: Microsoft to cut 2.1pc of its global workforce including some Australian roles, the ABC understands
item.description: ABC News understands Microsoft Australia staff will be impacted by the tech giant's latest round of lay-offs. Microsoft will cut about 2.1 per cent of its global workforce.

item.title: Bridget Archer as a stand in for Rockliff is a soft-launch succession plan for Tasmania's Liberals
item.description: Make no mistake, news that maverick Liberal Bridget Archer will be acting Tasmanian premier for a week is a significant step towards her eventually taking over from Jeremy Rockliff — a move sure to enrage those who believe they have a more rightful claim.

item.title: New steel plant for Newcastle nearly three decades on from BHP's closure
item.description: Once dubbed Australia's "steel city", steel production wound down in Newcastle in 1999 when BHP closed its plant. Now, an Australian company says it will build a new steel plant, powered only by electricity.

item.title: Former NRL player Jai Arrow marries partner months after motor neurone disease diagnosis
item.description: The former Rabbitohs and Queensland NRL player has married his partner Berina Colakovic in a small ceremony in Sydney, months after revealing his motor neurone disease diagnosis.

item.title: Richard White resigns as WiseTech chair but will remain on board and in executive role
item.description: The billionaire founder of WiseTech Global said he was stepping down as the company's chair after "recent personal media attention" created an "unnecessary distraction" from the business.

item.title: Man denied bail after drugs allegedly seized from vehicle in prison car park
item.description: Wayne Douglas Carnell has been remanded in custody after police allegedly found 100 grams of methamphetamine in his vehicle which was searched in a prison car park.

item.title: YouTube says video calling Bondi Beach terror attack a 'false flag' does not violate hate speech policies
item.description: YouTube's head of policy tells the Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion a video calling the Bondi Beach terror attack a "false flag" does not breach its hate speech policies.

item.title: ADFA cadet facing 79 additional charges after allegedly using hidden bathroom camera to film women
item.description: Police allege Tain Stephens was living at the Australian Defence Force Academy in Canberra when he hid a camera inside a soap dispenser in a female bathroom to capture intimate recordings of women.

item.title: AFL legend Tony Modra leaves hospital after truck crash near Victor Harbor
item.description: Former Adelaide Crows and Fremantle player Tony Modra says he has left hospital after he was seriously injured in a truck crash last month.

item.title: Why State of Origin legacies and futures are on the line for New South Wales in Game III
item.description: As New South Wales look to rise from the ashes and pull off a Lang Park Origin heist, the future of the team and the legacy of some of its greatest players will be tested in the furnace of a decider.

item.title: NRL announces new broadcast rights deal worth estimated $5.3 billion starting after 2027 season
item.description: As the NRL reveals its new broadcast rights deal worth billions of dollars over seven years in 2028 through 2034, executives try to hose down concerns about rising subscription prices.

item.title: With one missile, China sends world a reminder of its growing military power
item.description: This latest military test demonstrates China's growing willingness to use its military strength to send a warning to the Pacific: that Beijing now has the military and nuclear means to assert its dominance in the region.

item.title: Adelaide teen 'fight club' condemned by SA premier and police minister
item.description: South Australia's premier says he is appalled by an amateur children's "fight club", while the state's police minister says people who post videos of the fights online may face legal action on top of the participants in the bouts.

item.title: Government commits to restore current Paradise Dam wall over new build
item.description: The Queensland government announces a plan to rebuild the Paradise Dam wall where it is, despite its operator previously saying there were too many issues to fix it.

item.title: A frame-by-frame look at the tackle that earned Folarin Balogun his controversial red card
item.description: While many agree the red card call was too harsh, the story has become a bigger discussion about FIFA's integrity.

item.title: World Cup quick hits: American dream comes crashing down, Kylian Mbappé hits back at abuse
item.description: The US goes crashing out of its own World Cup after days of controversy and rancour, while Kylian Mbappé refuses to stand for dreadful racist abuse. Here are today's quick hits.

item.title: Fishing ban lifted early in SA amid positive signs for King George whiting
item.description: A temporary ban on recreational catches of King George whiting along parts of the SA coast is lifted earlier than scheduled, in a move that fishers say will benefit the whole sector.

item.title: Sports gambling company Betr granted Tasmanian gaming licence, amid inadequate regulation concerns
item.description: With sports bookmaker Betr granted a licence to operate in Tasmania, independent MP Andrew Wilkie slams the government for "rolling out the red carpet" despite the state admitting its regulations are not up to scratch.

item.title: Widow fights for answers over death of Laurence Harbeck on INPEX LNG plant
item.description: A Perth widow says she has been kept in the dark about the circumstances leading to the death of her husband as he worked on an offshore gas plant.

item.title: Qantas contacting more than 1 million customers over class action eligibility
item.description: More than 1 million Qantas customers are being notified they may be eligible for access to a multi-million-dollar settlement.

item.title: World Cup: Belgium consoles Folarin Balogun but claims 'justice' was served in 4-1 win over US
item.description: Belgium did not feel it was fair Folarin Balogun played for the US in the round of 16 match after he was given a red card in his team's previous match.

item.title: This Place: Stories of Gurlbin-wula
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Accused double killer Beau Lamarre-Condon's mother Coleen Lamarre granted bail
item.description: Coleen Lamarre has been in custody since April, when she was arrested in Sydney's inner west accused of attempting to influence a key witness in her son's upcoming double murder trial.

item.title: Launceston General Hospital emergency department overflowing, nurses union says
item.description: Launceston General Hospital's emergency department was overflowing on Monday night, with the nursing union describing the scenes as "chaotic" and "unsafe" — as Tasmania's health minister blames the federal government for a lack of action.

item.title: Suspected bird flu identified in giant petrel on SA coast, but testing to determine if H5
item.description: A suspected H5 bird flu case has been detected in a giant petrel on SA's Yorke Peninsula, but the state government says the viral load is low, with further results expected within the next 48 hours.

item.title: Territory Labor politician Manuel Brown dumped as opposition whip following speeding saga
item.description: Manuel Brown's dumping from the role comes after weeks of scrutiny over his driving record and confirmation he allowed two of his underage children to drive his personal car on an outstation.

item.title: A preliminary hearing for Charlie Kirk's alleged killer has begun. Here's what we learned
item.description: Utah prosecutors have begun presenting their case against Tyler James Robinson, the man accused of killing conservative activist Charlie Kirk, at a week-long preliminary trial.

item.title: ACT urged to make maths compulsory for year 11 and 12 students to ensure better long-term outcomes
item.description: A committee has found students in the ACT should study maths through to the end of year 12 to ensure they have better long-term outcomes, arguing a lack of maths understanding can exacerbate social inequality.

item.title: Officer filmed punching man during arrest was using an 'approved manoeuvre', NSW Police say
item.description: NSW Police say an officer filmed repeatedly punching a man in the back of the head on in Western Sydney on Monday night was using an "approved manoeuvre".

item.title: Brisbane woman calls for change after being harassed while running
item.description: A Brisbane mother says police can improve the way they respond to harassment complaints after she was followed by a car during her morning run.

item.title: Football club will hold service to honour Nathan Fitzgerald following fatal game day injury
item.description: The younger brother of footy player and teacher Nathan Fitzgerald, who died last night, has described him as a "gentle, wonderful" soul who cherished his family.

item.title: Perth man Lachie Perrem critically injured in robot vacuum fire
item.description: The family of a Perth man critically injured when a robot vacuum caught fire at his home say their lives have been "turned upside down" by the "horrific" incident.

item.title: Report suggesting DPP Sally Dowling be removed over media leak condemned by attorney-general
item.description: The inquiry into children's identity protection was called in October last year, but has focused almost solely on the leaking of the story to media.

item.title: Man held by security staff during alleged bottle shop assault and robbery at Northgate, north of Hobart
item.description: Security staff restrain a suspect during an alleged assault and robbery at a bottle shop in Hobart's north.

item.title: Piggery owners face court on animal cruelty charges
item.description: Five co-owners of an Adelaide Plains piggery are collectively accused of ill-treating their animals, including allegedly failing to ensure the animals had "adequate and appropriate living conditions".

item.title: Former chancellor Michael Still defends UOW-KordaMentha deal at ICAC hearing
item.description: An incoming vice-chancellor was providing input into a university review before his consulting firm later secured millions of dollars of related work, the ICAC has heard.

item.title: Homeless campers at Wagga Wagga under pressure as deadline to leave site arrives
item.description: People living at a homeless hotspot in Wagga were given until today to move on, but some say they still have nowhere to go.

item.title: Moira Deeming apologises to supporters in social media video amid Supreme Court stoush
item.description: The Liberal MP thanked her supporters and apologised for "distractions" in a video posted to her Facebook page, as questions still loom over her political future.

item.title: Tuesday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Toddler found in swimming pool and declared dead later found alive at hospital
item.description: Five hours after the young boy was pronounced dead next to a suburban swimming pool in the US state of Arizona, police were notified that the child was breathing again at a local hospital.

item.title: The world reacts to China’s missile test in the Pacific
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Behind the scenes of Trump’s untethered second term as president
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Is FIFA’s integrity at stake after Donald Trump’s interference?
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Moment of explosion in Damascus during Macron visit
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Explosions rock Damascus near hotel hosting French President Emmanuel Macron
item.description: French officials say Mr Macron did not hear the blasts, and his motorcade continued through the streets of the Syrian capital to a meeting with President Ahmed Al-Sharaa, in the first by a European head of state since 2024.

item.title: Ukrainian prosecutors confirm Monaco bomb suspect found dead
item.description: A Ukrainian woman suspected of a bomb attack that targeted a wealthy Ukrainian-born businessman in Monaco has been found shot dead in Ukraine, according to Ukraine's prosecutor general's office.

item.title: Marine Le Pen to enter French presidential race as court shortens ban on holding office
item.description: The court in Paris upheld Marine Le Pen's conviction for misusing EU funds but shortened her ban on running for elected office.

item.title: Prince Harry, Elton John and others lose privacy lawsuits against the Daily Mail
item.description: Prince Harry and six other claimants, including singer Elton John, have lost their claims against Daily Mail's publisher after alleging their phones were tapped, voicemails intercepted and personal information obtained through deception.

item.title: Reform UK leader Nigel Farage quits as an MP amid donation probe and vows to win by-election
item.description: Nigel Farage, the far-right political leader riding a surge of support in Britain, has announced a major gamble that could end his political career.

item.title: Live: Messi leads incredible Argentina comeback against Egypt, Switzerland faces Colombia
item.description: Lionel Messi guides Argentina to an incredible comeback win against Egypt, coming back from 2-0 down to steal a 3-2 win at the death. Follow live.

item.title: Donald Trump lashes out at NATO leaders, while $72bn in arms deals struck at summit
item.description: In a sign they are heeding calls from the United States to spend more to defend Europe, NATO leaders agree to deals worth about $72 billion.

item.title: Deadly podcasts to listen to this NAIDOC Week, from Indigenuity to Inside the Huddle
item.description: Some of our favourite Indigenous-led podcasts this week and every week include interviews with AFL greats, a dive into Aboriginal astronomy, and conversations with a renowned artist who loves Dolly Parton.

item.title: Travel quiz, July 8: Jump in, we're going on a trip around the world
item.description: Which Mediterranean island was a setting for The Godfather? And what's the capital city of Morocco?

item.title: PBS dispute could leave MS patients with huge bills for crucial drugs Ocrevus and Kesimpta
item.description: The latest PBS stoush with drugmakers means thousands of people with MS are at risk of being forced to pay $33,000 a year for lifesaving medication.

item.title: How this school cracked the code to close the gap in early reading
item.description: An inspiring turnaround at an Aboriginal community school could provide a scalable solution for closing the gap on Indigenous literacy.

item.title: Tens of thousands of mourners gather in Tehran for funeral of slain leader Ali Khamenei
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Three sons of Iran's slain leader Ali Khamenei appear at his funeral
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Video shows men who attended Khamenei's funeral and the son who didn't
item.description: The strictly choreographed funeral of Iran's former supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, has presented a rare insight into the image Tehran wants to project to the world and the men they see as central to it.

item.title: IS-linked Australian woman accused of beating enslaved Yazidi girl
item.description: The last of the known Islamic State-linked Australian women in Syria is expected to return home amid allegations a Yazidi girl was enslaved, beaten and raped in the woman's home a decade ago, the ABC can reveal.

item.title: What time is State of Origin III kick-off? How to watch the Maroons vs Blues decider
item.description: The men's State of Origin series will be decided tonight, as Queensland hosts New South Wales at Lang Park in a winner-takes-all Game III. Here is what you need to know ahead of kick-off.

item.title: Cancer survivor Torstein Træen claims Tour de France yellow jersey on desperately hot stage four
item.description: Torstein Træen was diagnosed with testicular cancer in 2022, leading to the removal of his left testicle. Three years later, he is the overall race leader at the Tour de France.

item.title: Teammates of Melbourne footballer Nathan Fitzgerald to sit out match after death
item.description: Epping football club's reserves players have decided to skip this weekend's game following the death of their beloved teammate Nathan Fitzgerald on Monday night.

item.title: Country footy player finds new sport after suffering eight concussions
item.description: Angus Pitman finds a new sporting passion after walking away from country footy due to coping eight concussions on the field.

item.title: For The People brand agency workers in battle to be paid amid liquidator investigations
item.description: Staff of an award-winning branding agency say they arrived at work one day to locked doors and a notice saying their employer was $25,000 behind on rent, and it went downhill from there.

item.title: Queensland government on track to make almost $5 billion a year from land tax as revenue grows
item.description: Over the next four years the government's annual revenue from the tax is forecast to climb by almost 80 per cent to $4.8 billion.

item.title: China missile launch tests Pacific relations as Australia looks to pounce
item.description: This week, perhaps for the first time in a long time, some sections of the Pacific are signalling a slight adjustment to their relationship with China.

item.title: Oral care giant Hismile used 'misleading' videos in social media advertisements
item.description: Oral care giant Hismile has used "misleading" videos in social media advertisements showing a sales professional, and its own staff, spruiking products while dressed as health workers.

item.title: Outback becomes oasis of birds, wildlife after 'unbelievable' desert rains
item.description: Winged explorers of all colours, shapes and sizes flock to the desert's edge to breed in the bustling wetlands of the Channel Country.
